title: Inhibitors of Nucleic Acid Synthesis
summary: المحاضرة دي بتغطي مجموعتين رئيسيتين بيمنعوا تركيب الأحماض النووية: أولاً (Quinolones) وخصوصاً (Fluoroquinolones) اللي بتشتغل بتثبيط (DNA gyrase) و(Topoisomerase IV) وبتقتل البكتيريا (Bactericidal). ثانياً (Folate Antagonists) وهي (Sulfonamides) و(Trimethoprim) و(Co-trimoxazole) اللي بتشتغل على مسار تركيب حمض الفوليك وبتوقف نمو البكتيريا (Bacteriostatic). التركيبة (Co-trimoxazole) هي الأقوى لأنها بتمنع خطوتين متتاليتين في نفس المسار مما يعطي تأثير (Synergistic). الكينولونات ممنوعة في الأطفال تحت 18 سنة والحامل والمرضع بسبب (Arthropathy).

key_drugs.name: Nalidixic acid
key_drugs.note: الجيل الأول، ضيق الطيف، ضد البكتيريا سالبة الجرام فقط

key_drugs.name: Ciprofloxacin
key_drugs.note: الأقوى ضد (Pseudomonas)، دواء الاختيار لعلاج والوقاية من (Anthrax)

key_drugs.name: Norfloxacin
key_drugs.note: فعّال في التهابات البول والبروستاتا، مستوياته في الدم منخفضة فلا يُستخدم في الإنتانات الجهازية

key_drugs.name: Ofloxacin
key_drugs.note: الوحيد الذي يخترق (CSF) جيداً، جرعة واحدة للسيلان

key_drugs.name: Levofloxacin
key_drugs.note: ممتاز لالتهابات الجهاز التنفسي و(S. pneumoniae)، أطول نصف عمر يسمح بجرعة يومية واحدة

key_drugs.name: Trovafloxacin
key_drugs.note: يسبب سُمية كبدية وكلوية، محجوز للحالات الشديدة داخل المستشفى فقط (≤14 يوم)

key_drugs.name: Moxifloxacin
key_drugs.note: يغطي اللاهوائيات و(Gram +ve)، لا يحتاج تعديل جرعة في الفشل الكلوي لأنه يُطرح كبدياً

key_drugs.name: Sulfadoxine
key_drugs.note: يُستخدم مع (Pyrimethamine) لعلاج الملاريا المقاومة للكلوروكين

key_drugs.name: Sulfasalazine
key_drugs.note: غير ممتص — يُستخدم في أمراض الأمعاء الالتهابية المزمنة (Ulcerative Colitis)

key_drugs.name: Trimethoprim
key_drugs.note: يثبط (Dihydrofolate reductase)، 20-50 مرة أقوى من السلفوناميدات، يُستخدم في التهابات البول والبروستاتا

key_drugs.name: Co-trimoxazole (TMP-SMX)
key_drugs.note: تركيبة (Sulfamethoxazole 400mg + Trimethoprim 80mg)، أفضل علاج لـ(Pneumocystis jirovecii) في مرضى الإيدز

mnemonics.title: قصة الـ QUEENS — ربط أجيال الكينولونات
mnemonics.body:
الكينولونات نربطها بكلمة QUEEN، وكل الأدوية تنتهي بـ (floxacin) نربطها بـ FLEXING يعني تفاخر — القريندات دايماً يتفاخروا!

 الجيل الثاني (2nd Generation):
الملكة اسمها نور = (Norfloxacin)
نور كانت ملكة صبورة = (Ciprofloxacin) — "صبور" بالعربي = Patient/Cipro
وعشان صبورة، هي OP قوية = (Ofloxacin + Pefloxacin)

 الجيل الثالث (3rd Generation):
الملكة دي تحب love = (Levofloxacin)
عندها قطة = (Gatifloxacin)
القطة تاكل صبار = (Sparfloxacin)

 الجيل الرابع (4th Generation):
الملكة اسمها ترف، يدلعوها "تروفة" = (Trovafloxacin)
وهي آخر كوين يعني الـ Maximum = (Moxifloxacin)

mnemonics.title: CANS — ربط موانع الكينولونات
mnemonics.body:
الكينولونات ممنوعة في مجموعة نسميها CANS:

C = Children (أقل من 18 سنة) — بتعمل (Arthropathy) تلف في الغضاريف
A = Arthropathy — سبب منع استخدامها في الأطفال أقل من 18 سنة (تلف الغضاريف)
N = iN pregnancy & lactation — ممنوع
S = Seizures — الكينولونات ترفع خطر النوبات، خصوصاً (Ciprofloxacin مع Theophylline)

ملاحظة: Antacids (الكالسيوم والمغنيسيوم والحديد والزنك) تقلل امتصاص الكينولونات — تجنّب إعطاءها معاً

تخيّل شخص في علبة قصدير (CAN) محبوس — محظور عليه الكينولون!

mnemonics.title: السلم المزدوج — كيف يشتغل Co-trimoxazole
mnemonics.body:
تخيّل البكتيريا بتصعد سلّم عشان تصنع (Folinic acid) — اللي هي الوقود للـ DNA.

السلم عنده درجتين:

 الدرجة الأولى: (PABA) → (Dihydrofolate) — الإنزيم = (Dihydropteroate Synthase)
 (Sulfonamides) بتكسر الدرجة دي — هي (PABA analogue) تنافسي

 الدرجة الثانية: (Dihydrofolate) → (Tetrahydrofolate) — الإنزيم = (Dihydrofolate Reductase)
 (Trimethoprim) بيكسر الدرجة دي

لما تجمعهم في (Co-trimoxazole) — بتكسر الدرجتين مع بعض!
النتيجة = تأثير (Synergistic) قوي جداً ومقاومة أبطأ بكتير

تذكّر: الإنسان بياخد (Folic acid) جاهزة من الأكل، فالدواء ما يأثرش عليه — ده سر الانتقائية!

mnemonics.title: CRUSH — الآثار الجانبية للسلفوناميدات
mnemonics.body:
آثار السلفوناميدات الجانبية نربطها بكلمة CRUSH:

C = Crystalluria — ترسّب البلورات في البول، عالجه بكثرة الماء وقلونة البول
R = Rash — طفح جلدي شائع
U = Urine displacement of bilirubin — (Kernicterus) في حديثي الولادة وأقل من شهرين
S = Stevens-Johnson Syndrome — طفح جلدي خطير ونادر ولكن شديد الخطورة
H = Hemolytic anemia — في مرضى نقص (G-6-PD)

ملاحظة: المرضى ذوو الأستلة البطيئة (Slow acetylators) أكثر عرضة للسمية، خاصة من Sulfapyridine

تخيّل السلفوناميدات بتـ'تكسر' (CRUSH) الجهاز الكلوي والدم والجلد!

mnemonics.title: Ciprofloxacin — الملك ضد Pseudomonas
mnemonics.body:
تخيّل (Ciprofloxacin) هو 'صبور الملك' — الملك الصبور والأقوى:

 دواء الاختيار لـ (Anthrax) — الجمرة الخبيثة
 الأقوى ضد (Pseudomonas) من بين كل الكينولونات
 بديل لـ (Aminoglycosides) أو بالتزامن مع (β-lactams)
 فعّال في إسهال المسافر و(TB) كعلاج مساعد

 لا يغطي (MRSA) و(Enterococci) و(Pneumococci) — 'المرض الصبور لا يستسلم للصبور!'

وتحذير مهم: (Ciprofloxacin + Theophylline) = خطر نوبات صرع!

mnemonics.title: تفاعلات الكينولونات الخطيرة — WTC
mnemonics.body:
WTC = الأدوية الي الكينولونات الجيل ٣ و٤ بترفع تركيزها:
- W = Warfarin (↑ خطر نزيف)
- T = Theophylline (سيبروفلوكساسين + Theophylline = تشنجات)
- C = Cyclosporine (↑ سمية كلوية)

mnemonics.title: أجيال الكوينولونات: QUEEN تتفاخر (flexing → -floxacin)
mnemonics.body: كل الأدوية تنتهي بـ -floxacin (flexing = الكوينز يتفاخرون). الجيل 2: ملكة اسمها نور (Norfloxacin)، صبورة (Ciprofloxacin)، وهي OP (Ofloxacin + Pefloxacin). الجيل 3: تحب love (Levofloxacin)، قطوتها (Gatifloxacin)، تأكل صبار (Sparfloxacin). الجيل 4: تُدلَّع تروفة (Trovafloxacin)، وهي الـ maximum (Moxifloxacin).

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of quinolones?
flashcards.back: Inhibit DNA Gyrase/Topoisomerase II (responsible for unwinding DNA in Gram-negative bacteria) and Topoisomerase IV (responsible for cell division in Gram-positive bacteria) — bactericidal

flashcards.front: Which quinolone is the drug of choice for treating and preventing Anthrax?
flashcards.back: Ciprofloxacin — also the most potent against Pseudomonas aeruginosa

flashcards.front: Why is Norfloxacin not used for systemic infections?
flashcards.back: Because its plasma levels are very low (insufficient plasma levels) — but it is effective for urinary tract infections and prostatitis

flashcards.front: Which quinolone is the only one that penetrates CSF well?
flashcards.back: Ofloxacin

flashcards.front: Which quinolones do not require dose adjustment in renal failure and why?
flashcards.back: Moxifloxacin — because it is primarily excreted via the liver (hepatic excretion) rather than the kidneys

flashcards.front: What are the 'Respiratory Fluoroquinolones' and why are they important?
flashcards.back: Levofloxacin, Trovafloxacin, Moxifloxacin, Gatifloxacin — used for respiratory tract infections due to S. pneumoniae. Note: Ciprofloxacin is NOT among them

flashcards.front: What are the contraindications to quinolones?
flashcards.back: Pregnancy, breastfeeding, children under 18 years (risk of Arthropathy). Also use caution in epileptic patients and avoid with antacids and minerals (iron, zinc, calcium)

flashcards.front: Which quinolones prolong QT interval and require caution in patients with arrhythmias?
flashcards.back: Sparfloxacin, Gatifloxacin, Levofloxacin, Moxifloxacin

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of sulfonamides?
flashcards.back: Structural analogue of PABA — competitively inhibits Dihydropteroate Synthase, thereby blocking folate synthesis → inhibits DNA and RNA synthesis. Bacteriostatic

flashcards.front: Why are sulfonamides selective for bacteria and do not affect human cells?
flashcards.back: Because human cells use preformed folic acid from the diet and do not synthesize it, whereas bacteria must synthesize it from PABA

flashcards.front: What is the use of Sulfasalazine and why?
flashcards.back: Treatment of Ulcerative Colitis — because it is not absorbed from the intestines, so it acts locally in the colon

flashcards.front: What is Kernicterus and how is it caused by sulfonamides?
flashcards.back: Sulfonamides displace Bilirubin from plasma proteins → free bilirubin rises → crosses the immature BBB in neonates → brain damage. Therefore contraindicated in infants under 2 months and in pregnant women near delivery

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Trimethoprim?
flashcards.back: Inhibits Dihydrofolate Reductase, which converts Dihydrofolate to Tetrahydrofolate/Folinic acid needed for DNA synthesis. 20-50 times more potent than sulfonamides

flashcards.front: What is the ratio of Co-trimoxazole and what is the rationale?
flashcards.back: Sulfamethoxazole 400 mg + Trimethoprim 80 mg (5:1 ratio) — acts on two sequential steps in the same pathway, producing a synergistic effect and slower resistance development

flashcards.front: What is the best treatment for Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia in AIDS patients?
flashcards.back: Co-trimoxazole (TMP-SMX) intravenously (IVI) — the most effective option

flashcards.front: What are the main side effects of Co-trimoxazole?
flashcards.back: Same as sulfonamides and Trimethoprim: Crystalluria, Hemolytic anemia (G6PD), Megaloblastic anemia (folate deficiency — treated with Folinic acid), Stevens-Johnson syndrome, Pancytopenia in AIDS patients

flashcards.front: What is quinolone resistance and how does it develop?
flashcards.back: 1. Altered target: mutations in DNA gyrase and Topoisomerase IV reduce drug binding. 2. Decreased accumulation: reduced outer membrane Porin proteins or increased efflux pumps

flashcards.front: What is the dangerous drug interaction between Ciprofloxacin and Theophylline?
flashcards.back: Ciprofloxacin inhibits Theophylline metabolism → elevated plasma levels → risk of seizures

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of fluoroquinolones?
flashcards.back: Inhibit DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV → concentration-dependent killing.

flashcards.front: What are the contraindications to fluoroquinolones?
flashcards.back: Pregnancy and children (cartilage damage) and risk of tendon rupture.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of sulfonamides?
flashcards.back: Inhibit dihydropteroate synthase (compete with PABA) → impaired folate synthesis.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Trimethoprim?
flashcards.back: Inhibits dihydrofolate reductase; combined with sulfonamides = sequential blockade (Co-trimoxazole).

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Rifampicin?
flashcards.back: Inhibits bacterial RNA polymerase; turns secretions orange-red and is a potent enzyme inducer.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Metronidazole?
flashcards.back: Converted to an active metabolite that cleaves DNA in anaerobes and protozoa; avoid alcohol (disulfiram reaction).

flashcards.front: What is the toxicity of sulfonamides?
flashcards.back: Stevens-Johnson syndrome rash, kernicterus in neonates, crystalluria.

flashcards.front: What is the interaction of fluoroquinolones with cations?
flashcards.back: Calcium/iron/aluminum reduce their absorption (chelation).

mcqs.q: Which of the following quinolones is the drug of choice for treating and preventing anthrax?
mcqs.explanation: Ciprofloxacin is the most potent against Pseudomonas and is the only approved drug for treating and preventing Anthrax

mcqs.q: The mechanism of action of sulfonamides is inhibition of:
mcqs.explanation: Sulfonamides are structural analogues of PABA and compete with it for Dihydropteroate synthase — while Trimethoprim inhibits Dihydrofolate reductase

mcqs.q: A 10-year-old child has acute urinary tract infection with Pseudomonas. Which statement is correct regarding quinolone treatment?
options: Give Ciprofloxacin at a reduced dose
Quinolones are fully contraindicated under 18 years except in exceptional cases
Levofloxacin is optimal for this age
mcqs.explanation: Quinolones are contraindicated in children under 18 years due to the risk of Arthropathy (cartilage damage) per standard guidelines, except in very specific situations

mcqs.q: Which quinolone is primarily excreted via the liver and does not require dose adjustment in renal failure?
mcqs.explanation: Moxifloxacin is primarily excreted via the liver, whereas most quinolones are renally excreted and require dose adjustment in renal failure

mcqs.q: A patient is taking Theophylline for asthma. Ciprofloxacin was added. What is the potential complication?
mcqs.explanation: Ciprofloxacin inhibits Theophylline metabolism → elevated plasma levels → risk of seizures

mcqs.q: Which sulfonamide is used for burn wound treatment and causes acid-base disturbances?
mcqs.explanation: Mafenide (sulfamafenide) is used topically for burns but causes pain and acid-base disturbances — while Silver Sulfadiazine is safer

mcqs.q: Co-trimoxazole is the best treatment for:
options: Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia in AIDS patients
mcqs.explanation: Co-trimoxazole is the most effective and approved treatment for Pneumocystis jirovecii (PCP) in AIDS patients via intravenous route

mcqs.q: A 3-week-old infant has an infection. Why are sulfonamides dangerous at this age?
options: They cause severe Crystalluria in children
They displace Bilirubin from proteins → Kernicterus
They cause Arthropathy in small joints
mcqs.explanation: Sulfonamides displace Bilirubin from plasma proteins → free bilirubin rises → crosses the immature BBB → Kernicterus (brainstem nuclear damage)

mcqs.q: Which fluoroquinolone is described as a 'Respiratory fluoroquinolone' effective against S. pneumoniae but with very poor activity against P. aeruginosa?
mcqs.explanation: Moxifloxacin is a 4th-generation respiratory fluoroquinolone, effective against anaerobes and Gram-positive bacteria, but has very poor activity against P. aeruginosa

mcqs.q: A patient on Warfarin was started on Co-trimoxazole. What is the expected effect?
options: Decreased Warfarin effect due to hepatic enzyme induction
Increased Warfarin levels due to displacement from plasma proteins
mcqs.explanation: Sulfonamides (in Co-trimoxazole) displace Warfarin from plasma proteins → elevated free Warfarin → increased bleeding risk
They prevent DNA unwinding and separation.

options: Sequential blockade of the folate pathway
mcqs.explanation: Two sequential steps in the folate pathway.

high_yield.point: Fluoroquinolones تثبّط DNA gyrase / topoisomerase IV → قاتلة، معتمدة على التركيز.
Sulfonamides تثبّط dihydropteroate synthase؛ Trimethoprim يثبّط dihydrofolate reductase → حصار تسلسلي للفولات.
Rifampicin يثبّط RNA polymerase الجرثومي؛ يصبغ الإفرازات برتقالي + محفّز إنزيمي قوي.
